title: Classification
description: Binary classification with logistic regression: turning a weighted sum into a probability with the sigmoid function, thresholding it into a class prediction, and evaluating the result.
objectives: Distinguish binary classification (predicting one of two classes) from regression (predicting a continuous number)
Compute the logistic regression pre-activation z = wx + b and its sigmoid probability sigma(z)
Apply a decision threshold to convert a predicted probability into a class prediction
Identify a classifier's decision boundary and interpret it geometrically
Evaluate a classifier's predictions against true labels using a confusion matrix, precision and recall

lessons.title: Binary classification: a category, not a number
Logistic regression: weighted sum, sigmoid, threshold
Decision boundaries and evaluating a classifier
